title: Dryopteris pseudodontoloma
taxonomy: Dryopteris pseudodontoloma belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This species is classified as a type of fern, specifically within the genus Dryopteris. The genus Dryopteris is part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. Within the classification, Dryopteris pseudodontoloma can be further identified by its unique characteristics, such as its frond shape and habitat. Its classification is based on a combination of morphological and molecular characteristics.

intro: Dryopteris pseudodontoloma, also known as the lesser scaly male fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain, inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of D. pseudodontoloma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, D. pseudodontoloma is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
